questions.question: What database does the project use?
What query builder or ORM does the project use for database access?
questions.notes: Changed from raw SQL to Knex in session 4

questions.question: What authentication method does the project use?
What is the JWT token expiry time?
What test framework does the project use?
What is the approximate test coverage?
What port does the server run on?
How are passwords stored?
What caching layer does the project use?
questions.notes: Redis in session 5, swapped to Memcached in session 6

questions.question: How does the project interact with the database — raw SQL or a query builder?
questions.notes: Raw SQL in sessions 1-3, Knex from session 4 onward

questions.question: How does the project handle user deletion?
questions.answer: Soft-delete pattern using a deleted_at column and POST /users/:id/deactivate endpoint
questions.notes: Hard delete in sessions 1-8, soft-delete from session 9

questions.question: What environment variable configures the cache connection?
questions.notes: REDIS_URL in session 5, renamed to CACHE_URL in session 6

questions.question: Does the project have a DELETE /users/:id endpoint?
questions.answer: No, it was removed in favor of soft-delete via POST /users/:id/deactivate

questions.question: Does the project use the sqlite3 npm package directly?
questions.answer: No, it uses Knex with better-sqlite3

questions.question: What CI/CD pipeline does the project use?
What cloud provider hosts the project?
What logging framework does the project use?
What is a known pitfall with the authentication system?
questions.answer: The ignoreExpiration flag was accidentally left enabled, causing expired tokens to be accepted. Development-only flags must be removed before committing.

questions.question: Why was Redis replaced?
questions.answer: Redis licensing concerns — Redis changed to SSPL license which was incompatible with deployment requirements

questions.question: What performance issue was found and fixed in the users endpoint?
questions.answer: N+1 query problem when fetching users with roles — fixed with a LEFT JOIN and GROUP_CONCAT
